verification_note: Reviewed against current community-maintained Elite Dangerous references on 2026-04-12. Entries with weak or conflicting evidence were removed or softened rather than guessed.

encoded.name: Jameson's Crashed Cobra
encoded.method: Scan the 4 data points at the crash site, relog, then trade down at an encoded material trader
encoded.note: This is a strong general encoded farm, but it does not directly drop wake-scan-only materials such as Datamined Wake Exceptions.

encoded.name: High Wake Scans
encoded.method: Scan high wakes in busy systems near stations or engineer hubs
encoded.note: Use a wake scanner. This remains the direct method for wake-scan materials.

encoded.name: HGE Pharmaceutical Isolators
encoded.method: Search high-population Outbreak systems for High Grade Emissions, then collect and trade as needed

raw.name: Crystalline Shards
raw.method: Shoot crystal shards and collect with SRV, or use ship-based collection where preferred
raw.note: These are the commonly verified shard locations for the main G4 raws. Selenium is not part of the standard crystal shard route.

raw.name: Brain Trees (Selenium focus)
raw.method: Harvest brain trees for Selenium; flak plus collector limpets or SRV collection both work depending on preference
raw.note: Selenium farming is slower than the main crystal shard route.

raw.name: Crashed Anaconda
raw.method: Shoot cargo racks and collect with SRV
raw.note: Kept only the best-known entry. Other specific crashed Anaconda sites from the original file were not kept because I could not verify them well enough.

manufactured.name: High Grade Emissions (HGE)
manufactured.method: Use a ship with collector limpets, search high-pop systems, drop into HGE, collect, then trade down at a manufactured trader
manufactured.note: The original state mapping had multiple errors.

manufactured.name: Dav's Hope
manufactured.method: Drive the abandoned settlement loop in an SRV, relog, repeat
manufactured.note: Still widely used as a general manufactured-material loop, though HGEs are usually faster for targeted G5 farming.

manufactured.name: Combat / CZ / bounty cleanup
manufactured.method: Low/High CZs, RES sites, pirate massacre loops, then scoop salvage

odyssey_onfoot.name: Arai's Mine
odyssey_onfoot.method: Loot the settlement; disable alarms if needed
odyssey_onfoot.note: The original body was wrong. Iah Bulu remains a commonly recommended Odyssey farming system.

odyssey_onfoot.name: Pirate Attack settlements
odyssey_onfoot.method: Use the galaxy map or external tools to find factions in Pirate Attack state and farm settlements there
odyssey_onfoot.note: Good category to keep, but the exact best system changes over time.

guardian.name: Module Blueprint Site
guardian.method: Activate pylons, scan the data terminal, kill sentinels, relog as needed

guardian.name: Weapon Blueprint Site
guardian.method: Activate pylons, scan terminal, kill sentinels

guardian.name: Vessel Blueprint Site
guardian.method: Activate pylons and scan the terminal; some runs also use an Ancient Key route depending on site procedure

guardian.name: Obelisk Data
guardian.method: Scan active Guardian obelisks and relog
guardian.note: I removed the original specific obelisk systems because I could not verify those exact entries strongly enough from the sources I checked.

thargoid.name: Scout NHSS
thargoid.method: Fight scouts in NHSS and collect dropped materials

thargoid.name: Titan / Maelstrom tissue collection
thargoid.method: Use the appropriate AX/titan collection route for tissue samples

bgs_tips.method: Prioritize high-population systems and use material traders aggressively instead of trying to farm every exact material directly.
